<!-- Generated by `python -m causal_shap.build glossary` from data/glossary.yml. Do not edit. -->

::: {.glossary-group}
### Attribution

<dl class="glossary">
<dt>asymmetric Shapley values</dt><dd>Shapley values restricted to coalitions consistent with a causal ordering.</dd>
<dt>interventional SHAP</dt><dd>SHAP whose value function drops features by intervention (marginal), not conditioning.</dd>
<dt>PBI</dt><dd>Proximity Bias Index — how much attribution mass concentrates on nodes near the outcome.</dd>
<dt>SHAP</dt><dd>Shapley Additive exPlanations — feature attributions from cooperative game theory.</dd>
<dt>structural Causal SHAP</dt><dd>Attribution whose value function is E[f(X) | do(X_S = x_S)] under a known structural model, propagating interventions through descendants.</dd>
</dl>
:::

::: {.glossary-group}
### Discovery

<dl class="glossary">
<dt>causal sufficiency</dt><dd>The assumption that there are no unmeasured common causes.</dd>
<dt>CPDAG</dt><dd>Completed Partially Directed Acyclic Graph — the output of PC; an equivalence class of DAGs where some edges stay undirected.</dd>
<dt>faithfulness</dt><dd>Statistical dependencies reflect causal structure with no coincidental cancellations.</dd>
<dt>GES</dt><dd>A score-based discovery algorithm that greedily maximizes a BIC score.</dd>
<dt>LiNGAM</dt><dd>A functional-causal-model method assuming linear, non-Gaussian mechanisms.</dd>
<dt>Markov condition</dt><dd>Each variable is independent of its non-descendants given its parents.</dd>
<dt>NOTEARS</dt><dd>A continuous-optimization discovery method with a differentiable acyclicity constraint.</dd>
<dt>PC</dt><dd>A constraint-based discovery algorithm using conditional-independence tests.</dd>
<dt>skeleton F1</dt><dd>F1 between two graphs' undirected edge sets (direction ignored).</dd>
</dl>
:::

::: {.glossary-group}
### Estimation

<dl class="glossary">
<dt>ATC</dt><dd>Average Treatment effect on the Controls.</dd>
<dt>ATE</dt><dd>Average Treatment Effect across the whole population.</dd>
<dt>ATT</dt><dd>Average Treatment effect on the Treated.</dd>
<dt>CATE</dt><dd>Conditional Average Treatment Effect within a covariate-defined subgroup.</dd>
<dt>estimand</dt><dd>The causal quantity you intend to estimate, defined before any model.</dd>
</dl>
:::

::: {.glossary-group}
### Identification

<dl class="glossary">
<dt>adjustment set</dt><dd>A set of variables that blocks every backdoor path.</dd>
<dt>backdoor path</dt><dd>A non-causal path from exposure to outcome that must be blocked.</dd>
</dl>
:::

::: {.glossary-group}
### Structure

<dl class="glossary">
<dt>chain</dt><dd>X -> Y -> Z; information flows through Y, and conditioning on Y blocks it.</dd>
<dt>collider</dt><dd>A node caused by two or more variables. Conditioning on it opens a spurious path and induces selection bias.</dd>
<dt>confounder</dt><dd>A common cause of both exposure and outcome; adjust for it.</dd>
<dt>DAG</dt><dd>Directed Acyclic Graph. Directed: arrows go from cause to effect. Acyclic: no feedback loops.</dd>
<dt>edge</dt><dd>An arrow representing a direct causal influence between two nodes.</dd>
<dt>exposure</dt><dd>The treatment or intervention whose effect is of interest.</dd>
<dt>fork</dt><dd>X <- Y -> Z; Y is a common cause, and conditioning on Y blocks the path.</dd>
<dt>mediator</dt><dd>A node on the causal path from exposure to outcome; adjust with care.</dd>
<dt>node</dt><dd>A variable in the graph (exposure, outcome, or covariate).</dd>
<dt>outcome</dt><dd>The endpoint being explained or predicted.</dd>
<dt>proxy</dt><dd>A variable predictive of the outcome but with no total causal effect on it, typically a descendant or co-descendant of the outcome.</dd>
</dl>
:::
